steps.frontend: Call useVoidInvoice(id, { reason }). Show loading state on confirm button.
steps.backend: POST /api/invoices/:id/void. Guard: status must not be 'voided' or 'paid'. Set status='voided'. Remove all PaymentApplications linked to this invoice (restore payment unapplied balance). If journal entry exists, create reversing entry. Record reason in audit_log.
steps.cache: Invalidate ['invoices', orgId], ['invoice', id], ['payments', orgId] (payment unapplied balances changed), ['journal_entries', orgId]

steps.name: Display Voided State
steps.frontend: Navigate back to invoice detail. Show 'VOIDED' watermark badge (red, large). Disable all action buttons except 'Download PDF'. Show voided reason in audit trail section.

flows.journal_entry_side_effects: Reversing journal entry created (DR Revenue accounts, CR Accounts Receivable) dated today.
postconditions: Reversal journal entry created if original entry existed

error_paths.resolution: Require explicit confirmation with refund acknowledgement
Rollback void; show error

rollback_steps: Void and reversal journal entry are atomic — rollback both on failure

success_state.ui_change: Status badge voided; all action buttons hidden except Download

flows.name: Void Bill

steps.name: Initiate Void
steps.trigger: User clicks 'Void Bill' row_action. Only available when status IN ('draft','received','approved','partial').
steps.frontend: Open ConfirmDialog: 'Void bill BIL-0010? Any recorded payments will need to be re-applied.' Require reason input.

steps.name: Process Void
steps.frontend: Call useVoidBill(id, { reason }).
steps.backend: POST /api/bills/:id/void. Guard: status must not be 'voided' or 'paid'. Set status='voided'. Remove linked PaymentApplications, restore payment unapplied balance. Create reversing journal entry. Record in audit_log.

steps.name: Display Voided State
steps.frontend: Show 'VOIDED' badge. Disable edit/approve/payment actions. Show void reason in audit trail.

flows.journal_entry_side_effects: Reversing journal entry created (DR Accounts Payable, CR Expense accounts) dated today.

error_paths.resolution: Require explicit confirmation
Rollback void

flows.name: Onboard New Organization

steps.name: Register Organization
steps.frontend: POST /api/auth/register (outside spec scope — handled by provisioning service). On success, org and admin User created. Access token returned.
steps.backend: Create Organization row with slug=slugify(name), currency, timezone. Create User with role='admin'. Seed default chart of accounts from seeds/chart_of_accounts.sql. Seed default tax rates.
steps.cache: Set authStore with new user + org

steps.name: Configure Organization Settings
steps.frontend: Pre-filled form with name, currency, timezone, fiscal_year_start_month. Show 'Setup Checklist' widget (chart of accounts ✓, tax rates, invite users, open first period).
steps.backend: PATCH /api/organizations/:id

steps.name: Review Chart of Accounts
steps.frontend: Show default seeded accounts in hierarchy tree view. Admin can add/edit/deactivate accounts. Wizard suggests common additions by industry type.
steps.backend: GET /api/accounts/tree

steps.name: Configure Tax Rates
steps.frontend: Show default seeded tax rates. Admin reviews and adds org-specific rates.
steps.backend: GET /api/tax-rates, POST /api/tax-rates

steps.name: Create First Financial Period
steps.frontend: Form to create fiscal year period (start_date, end_date). System can auto-generate 12 monthly sub-periods.
steps.backend: POST /api/financial-periods

steps.name: Invite Team Members
steps.trigger: Admin navigates to /admin/users and clicks Invite User
steps.frontend: SlideOverForm: email, role selector, optional name. Send invite email with magic link.
steps.backend: POST /api/users/invite. Create User with status='invited'. Send email with 24h signup token.

preconditions: No existing org for this email domain (if enforced)

error_paths.resolution: Show login link
Block org creation; show retry

rollback_steps: Full rollback if any seeding step fails — do not leave partial orgs

flows.name: Invite and Onboard User

steps.name: Admin Sends Invite
steps.frontend: Open SlideOverForm. Fields: email (required), role (required), first_name, last_name (optional).
steps.backend: POST /api/users/invite. Create User(status='invited'). Generate 24h invite token (Redis). Send invite email with link /accept-invite?token=<token>.

steps.name: User Accepts Invite
steps.frontend: Navigate to /accept-invite?token=<token>. Show set-password form with org name and inviter name.
steps.backend: Validate token. POST /api/auth/accept-invite { token, password }. Set User.status='active', hash password, issue JWT.

steps.name: First Login
steps.frontend: Show onboarding tooltip overlay if user is new (first_login=true). Highlight sidebar modules available to their role.

preconditions: Invitee email not already a member
postconditions: Invite email sent with token (expires 7 days)

error_paths.resolution: Show error: user already member
User still created; show manual invite link in admin UI

rollback_steps: If email fails, keep User record but mark invite_email_error=true

flows.name: Manage Chart of Accounts

steps.name: View Account Tree
steps.frontend: Render collapsible tree using GET /api/accounts/tree. Show account code, name, type, balance. Toggle between tree view and flat table view.
steps.backend: GET /api/accounts/tree

steps.name: Create Account
steps.frontend: Open SlideOverForm. Fields: code (unique), name, type (enum), subtype, parent_id (AccountSelector filtered to same type), description, currency, is_active. Validate code uniqueness client-side via debounced check.
steps.backend: POST /api/accounts. Validate code unique in org.

steps.name: Edit Account
steps.trigger: User clicks edit on account row
steps.frontend: Pre-filled SlideOverForm. Cannot change type after account has transactions.
steps.backend: PATCH /api/accounts/:id. Guard: cannot change type if account has journal lines.

steps.name: Deactivate Account
steps.trigger: User clicks Deactivate in account row actions
steps.frontend: ConfirmDialog: 'Deactivate account [code] [name]? It will no longer appear in transaction selectors.'
steps.backend: PATCH /api/accounts/:id { is_active: false }. Guard: cannot deactivate if account has open transactions.

cross_entity_updates: AccountSelector components re-fetch to exclude inactive accounts
preconditions: User has accounts.create or accounts.edit permission
postconditions: Account created/updated; parent-child relationships maintained; code uniqueness enforced

error_paths.resolution: Server 409; show field-level error on code input
Block; validate server-side that parent chain has no cycle
Block hard delete; only soft delete; warn about existing journal lines

flows.name: Generate Financial Report

steps.name: Select Report Type
steps.frontend: Show report cards: Trial Balance, Balance Sheet, Income Statement, General Ledger, Cash Flow Statement, Aged Receivables, Aged Payables. Each shows description and required parameters.

steps.name: Set Parameters
steps.frontend: Show parameter form. Common: as_of_date (DatePicker) or date_from/date_to (DateRangePicker), compare_prior_period (Toggle), currency. General Ledger adds account_id filter.

steps.name: Run Report
steps.frontend: Show LoadingSkeleton. Call report hook (e.g. useTrialBalance({ as_of_date })).
steps.backend: GET /api/reports/trial-balance (or other endpoint). Heavy queries use pg materialized view or CTEs. Response includes report_data + metadata (generated_at, period, org).
steps.cache: staleTime 300000 (5 min). query_key includes all parameters.

steps.name: Export Report
steps.trigger: User clicks Export CSV or Export PDF
steps.frontend: Call report endpoint with Accept: text/csv or Accept: application/pdf. Trigger browser download via blob URL.
steps.backend: Same endpoint, content negotiation via Accept header. PDF uses Puppeteer or weasyprint server-side.

preconditions: Posted journal entries exist for selected period
postconditions: Report data rendered from cache or computed on-demand

error_paths.resolution: Show empty state with explanation
Trigger on-demand computation; show loading state

success_state.ui_change: Report table populated; export buttons enabled

flows.name: Approve Expense

steps.name: Bookkeeper Submits Expense
steps.trigger: Bookkeeper opens expense draft and clicks Submit for Approval
steps.frontend: Call useSubmitExpense(id). status: draft → submitted.
steps.backend: PATCH /api/expenses/:id { status: 'submitted' } or POST /api/expenses/:id/submit. Notify users with expenses.approve permission via WebSocket.

steps.name: Manager Reviews
steps.trigger: Manager sees notification or visits /expenses filtered to status=submitted
steps.frontend: Show expense detail with amount, description, category, receipt. Show Approve and Reject action buttons (visible only to roles with expenses.approve).

steps.name: Approve
steps.frontend: Call useApproveExpense(id). Show success toast 'Expense approved'.
steps.backend: POST /api/expenses/:id/approve. Set status='approved'. Create journal entry: DR Expense Account, CR Accounts Payable or Cash.

steps.name: Reject
steps.frontend: Open ConfirmDialog with required reason textarea. Call useRejectExpense(id, { reason }).
steps.backend: POST /api/expenses/:id/reject. Set status='draft'. Notify submitter with reason.

flows.journal_entry_side_effects: On approve: DR Expense account, CR AP or Cash account

error_paths.resolution: Idempotent — return current state

flows.name: Upload Attachment

steps.name: Open Attachment Panel
steps.trigger: User opens a detail page (Invoice, Bill, Expense, Journal Entry). Attachment panel shown in right sidebar.
steps.frontend: useAttachments({ entity_type, entity_id }) fetches existing attachments. Shows thumbnail grid with file name, size, upload date.
steps.backend: GET /api/attachments?entity_type=invoice&entity_id=:id

steps.name: Upload File
steps.trigger: User drags file or clicks upload zone in AttachmentUploader component
steps.frontend: Validate client-side: file type in [PDF, PNG, JPG, HEIC], size < 20MB. Show upload progress bar. Call useCreateAttachment().
steps.backend: POST /api/attachments (multipart/form-data). Backend uploads to S3 bucket (key: {org_id}/{entity_type}/{entity_id}/{uuid}.{ext}). Create Attachment row. Return IAttachment.

steps.name: Download / Preview
steps.frontend: Call useAttachment(id) to get signed URL. Open PDF in new tab or show image in lightbox. Download button triggers blob download.
steps.backend: GET /api/attachments/:id generates signed S3 URL (expires 15min).

steps.name: Delete Attachment
steps.trigger: User clicks delete icon on thumbnail
steps.frontend: ConfirmDialog: 'Delete this file? This cannot be undone.'
steps.backend: DELETE /api/attachments/:id. Soft-delete Attachment row (sets deleted_at). S3 object retained for 30 days then purged by lifecycle rule.

postconditions: Entity detail page shows attachment in AttachmentPanel

error_paths.resolution: Client-side validation before upload; show error
Mark attachment quarantined; show warning in AttachmentPanel; prevent download

rollback_steps: If step 3 confirm fails, mark attachment as failed and clean up S3 object asynchronously

flows.name: Manage Tax Rates

steps.name: View Tax Rates
steps.frontend: Table of tax rates: name, rate %, tax_type, is_active. useTaxRates().
steps.backend: GET /api/tax-rates

steps.name: Create Tax Rate
steps.frontend: SlideOverForm. Fields: name (required), rate (number 0-100, required), tax_type (Select), description, is_active (Toggle default true).
steps.backend: POST /api/tax-rates.

steps.name: Edit Tax Rate
steps.frontend: Pre-filled SlideOverForm. Warn if rate has been used on existing lines: 'Changing rate will not affect past transactions.'
steps.backend: PATCH /api/tax-rates/:id.

steps.name: Deactivate Tax Rate
steps.frontend: Optimistic update — immediately hide from TaxRateSelector. Can be reactivated.
steps.backend: PATCH /api/tax-rates/:id { is_active: false }.

cross_entity_updates: TaxRateSelector re-fetches to exclude inactive rates
flows.journal_entry_side_effects: None — tax rates are configuration, not transactions
preconditions: User has tax_rates.create or tax_rates.edit permission
postconditions: TaxRate created/updated; available on invoice/bill/expense line items

error_paths.resolution: Zod validation 0-100; show field error
Warn: existing line items retain rate; new items cannot use it

flows.name: Search and Filter Records

steps.name: Global Search
steps.trigger: User presses Cmd+K (or clicks search icon in Header)
steps.frontend: Open CommandPalette (Radix Dialog). SearchInput with debounced 300ms. useGlobalSearch(q) fires when q.length >= 2. Display results grouped by entity type with icon, label, secondary info.
steps.backend: GET /api/search?q={q}

steps.name: Navigate to Result
steps.frontend: Navigate to entity detail page (/invoices/:id, /accounts/:id etc). Close CommandPalette.

steps.name: Entity-level Filter
steps.trigger: User is on a list page (e.g. /invoices) and uses filter bar
steps.frontend: FilterBar component reads URL search params. Each field maps to a param: ?status=sent&customer_id=xxx&date_from=2025-01-01. useSearchParams() syncs state. FilterStore not used — URL is source of truth.
steps.backend: GET /api/invoices?status=sent&customer_id=xxx&date_from=2025-01-01

steps.name: Reset Filters
steps.frontend: setSearchParams({}) clears all params except page=1. List refetches with no filters.

postconditions: Filtered results displayed; URL updated with filter state for shareability

error_paths.resolution: Show EmptyState variant=no_results with Clear Filters action
Show EmptyState variant=error with Retry

success_state.ui_change: Table updates; URL bar reflects filter state; result count shown

flows.name: Create and Pay Bill

steps.name: Create Bill
steps.frontend: Navigate to /bills/new. Form: VendorSelector (required), bill_date, due_date, bill_number (optional), currency, notes. BillLines sub-form: AccountSelector, description, quantity, unit_price, TaxRateSelector.

steps.name: Save Bill
steps.frontend: useCreateBill(). status='draft'.
steps.backend: POST /api/bills. Auto-generate bill_number. Compute totals.

steps.name: Submit for Approval (optional)
steps.frontend: useUpdateBill(id, { status: 'pending_approval' }). If org has no approval workflow, skip to step 4.
steps.backend: PATCH /api/bills/:id. Notify users with bills.approve.

steps.name: Approve Bill
steps.frontend: useApproveBill(id). status: pending_approval → approved.
steps.backend: POST /api/bills/:id/approve. Create GL journal entry: DR Expense accounts, CR Accounts Payable.

steps.name: Record Payment
steps.trigger: User clicks Record Payment on approved bill
steps.frontend: Open SlideOverForm. Fields: amount (pre-filled with balance_due), payment_date, method, bank_account_id, reference. useCreatePayment({ direction: 'outbound', ... }). Then useCreatePaymentApplication({ payment_id, entity_type: 'bill', entity_id: bill.id, amount_applied }).
steps.backend: POST /api/payments + POST /api/payment-applications. Update bill.amount_paid, recalculate balance_due, set status='paid' if fully paid.

flows.journal_entry_side_effects: On approve: DR Expense accounts, CR Accounts Payable. On payment: DR Accounts Payable, CR Cash/Bank.
postconditions: Bill created → approved → payment recorded → bill marked paid

error_paths.resolution: Allow; create overpayment credit on vendor
Allow payment in current open period even if bill period is locked

rollback_steps: Payment and bill status update are atomic

computed_fields.formula: quantity * unit_price
computed_fields.notes: Computed at DB level via generated column. Also computed client-side for real-time preview.

computed_fields.formula: quantity * unit_cost
SUM(invoice_lines.line_total)
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT SUM(line_total) FROM invoice_lines WHERE invoice_id = :id
computed_fields.notes: Recomputed server-side on any line mutation and stored on invoice row.

computed_fields.formula: SUM(invoice_lines.line_total * tax_rates.percentage / 100)
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT SUM(il.line_total * tr.percentage / 100) FROM invoice_lines il LEFT JOIN tax_rates tr ON il.tax_rate_id = tr.id WHERE il.invoice_id = :id

computed_fields.formula: subtotal + tax_total
computed_fields.db_formula: subtotal + tax_total (stored column, updated on line mutations)

computed_fields.formula: total - SUM(payments.amount WHERE invoice_id = id AND NOT deleted)
computed_fields.db_formula: total - COALESCE((SELECT SUM(amount) FROM payments WHERE invoice_id = :id AND deleted_at IS NULL), 0)
computed_fields.notes: Stored on invoice row for query performance. Recomputed on any payment mutation.

computed_fields.formula: SUM(bill_lines.line_total)
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT SUM(line_total) FROM bill_lines WHERE bill_id = :id

computed_fields.formula: SUM(bill_lines.line_total * tax_rates.percentage / 100)
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT SUM(bl.line_total * tr.percentage / 100) FROM bill_lines bl LEFT JOIN tax_rates tr ON bl.tax_rate_id = tr.id WHERE bl.bill_id = :id

computed_fields.formula: subtotal + tax_total
total - SUM(payments.amount WHERE bill_id = id AND NOT deleted)
computed_fields.db_formula: total - COALESCE((SELECT SUM(amount) FROM payments WHERE bill_id = :id AND deleted_at IS NULL), 0)

computed_fields.formula: SUM(journal_lines.debit WHERE journal_entry_id = id)
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT SUM(debit) FROM journal_lines WHERE journal_entry_id = :id

computed_fields.formula: SUM(journal_lines.credit WHERE journal_entry_id = id)
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT SUM(credit) FROM journal_lines WHERE journal_entry_id = :id

computed_fields.formula: SUM(journal_lines.debit) - SUM(journal_lines.credit) WHERE account_id = id AND journal_entry.status = posted
computed_fields.ts_formula: Fetched from server — never computed client-side
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN type IN ('asset','expense') THEN debit - credit ELSE credit - debit END) FROM journal_lines jl JOIN journal_entries je ON jl.journal_entry_id = je.id WHERE jl.account_id = :id AND je.status = 'posted'
computed_fields.notes: Normal balance direction depends on account type. Asset/Expense: debit increases. Liability/Equity/Revenue: credit increases.
computed_fields.sign_convention: positive = normal balance for account type (debit-normal for asset/expense; credit-normal for liability/equity/revenue)

computed_fields.formula: opening_balance + SUM(bank_transactions.amount WHERE direction=credit) - SUM(bank_transactions.amount WHERE direction=debit) AND reconciliation_status != excluded
computed_fields.ts_formula: Fetched from server — never computed client-side
computed_fields.db_formula: opening_balance + COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN direction='credit' THEN amount ELSE -amount END) FROM bank_transactions WHERE bank_account_id = :id AND reconciliation_status != 'excluded', 0)

computed_fields.formula: BankAccount.current_balance as of session.end_date (sum of cleared transactions up to end_date)
computed_fields.db_formula: opening_balance + SUM(CASE WHEN direction='credit' THEN amount ELSE -amount END) FROM bank_transactions WHERE bank_account_id = :bank_account_id AND transaction_date <= :end_date AND reconciliation_status = 'cleared'

computed_fields.formula: statement_ending_balance - book_ending_balance
computed_fields.db_formula: statement_ending_balance - book_ending_balance (stored, updated when book_ending_balance changes)
computed_fields.notes: Must equal 0 before session can be marked completed.

computed_fields.formula: SUM(invoices.balance_due WHERE customer_id = id AND status NOT IN ['voided','paid'])
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(balance_due), 0) FROM invoices WHERE customer_id = :id AND status NOT IN ('voided','paid') AND deleted_at IS NULL
computed_fields.notes: Sum of balance_due across all non-voided, non-paid invoices for this customer. Excludes written_off invoices.

computed_fields.formula: SUM(bills.balance_due WHERE vendor_id = id AND status NOT IN ['voided','paid'])
computed_fields.db_formula: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(balance_due), 0) FROM bills WHERE vendor_id = :id AND status NOT IN ('voided','paid') AND deleted_at IS NULL
computed_fields.notes: Sum of balance_due across all non-voided, non-paid bills for this vendor.

computed_fields.formula: SUM(journal_lines.debit WHERE account_id = id AND journal_entry.status = 'posted')
computed_fields.description: Total posted debits on this account (not net)

computed_fields.formula: SUM(journal_lines.credit WHERE account_id = id AND journal_entry.status = 'posted')
computed_fields.description: Total posted credits on this account (not net)

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(accounts WHERE parent_account_id = id AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Number of direct child accounts

computed_fields.formula: total - balance_due
computed_fields.description: Total amount already paid against this invoice

computed_fields.formula: status NOT IN ('paid','voided','written_off') AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE
computed_fields.description: True when unpaid and past due date

computed_fields.formula: CASE WHEN is_overdue THEN CURRENT_DATE - due_date ELSE 0 END
computed_fields.description: Number of calendar days past the due date

computed_fields.formula: total - balance_due
computed_fields.description: Total amount already paid against this bill

computed_fields.formula: status NOT IN ('paid','voided') AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE
computed_fields.description: True when unpaid and past due date

computed_fields.formula: CASE WHEN is_overdue THEN CURRENT_DATE - due_date ELSE 0 END
computed_fields.description: Number of calendar days past the due date

computed_fields.formula: total_debit = total_credit
computed_fields.description: True when debits equal credits — required before posting

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(journal_lines WHERE journal_entry_id = id AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Number of journal lines on this entry

computed_fields.formula: amount - SUM(payment_applications.applied_amount WHERE payment_id = id AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Portion of payment not yet applied to an invoice or bill

computed_fields.formula: unapplied_amount <= 0
computed_fields.description: True when the full payment amount has been applied

computed_fields.formula: SUM(invoices.total WHERE customer_id = id AND status NOT IN ('voided','written_off') AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Lifetime invoiced amount for this customer

computed_fields.formula: SUM(payments.amount WHERE customer_id = id AND direction = 'inbound' AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Lifetime payments received from this customer

computed_fields.formula: SUM(bills.total WHERE vendor_id = id AND status NOT IN ('voided') AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Lifetime billed amount from this vendor

computed_fields.formula: SUM(payments.amount WHERE vendor_id = id AND direction = 'outbound' AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Lifetime payments made to this vendor

computed_fields.formula: EXISTS(reconciliation_matches WHERE bank_transaction_id = id AND status = 'confirmed' AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: True when this transaction has a confirmed reconciliation match

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(reconciliation_matches WHERE session_id = id AND status = 'confirmed' AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Number of confirmed matched transactions in this session

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(bank_transactions WHERE bank_account_id = session.bank_account_id AND created_at BETWEEN session.start_date AND session.end_date) - matched_count
computed_fields.description: Number of unmatched bank transactions in this session window

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(journal_entries WHERE period_id = id AND status = 'posted' AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Number of posted journal entries in this period

computed_fields.formula: amount * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate.percentage, 0) / 100)
computed_fields.description: Gross amount including applicable tax

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(invoice_lines WHERE tax_rate_id = id AND deleted_at IS NULL) + COUNT(bill_lines WHERE tax_rate_id = id AND deleted_at IS NULL) + COUNT(expenses WHERE tax_rate_id = id AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Total number of line items referencing this tax rate

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(users WHERE org_id = id AND status = 'active' AND deleted_at IS NULL)
computed_fields.description: Number of currently active users in this organization

domain_rules: Double-entry invariant: every posted JournalEntry MUST have SUM(debit) = SUM(credit) — enforced before commit
JournalEntries in a locked FinancialPeriod cannot be posted or voided
JournalLines cannot be edited once parent JournalEntry is posted — must void and re-enter
Account codes must be unique per org; format validated against org account_code_pattern setting
Voiding creates a full mirror entry with all amounts negated; original marked voided
Normal balance: asset/expense = debit_normal; liability/equity/revenue = credit_normal
RecurringTemplates must have balanced template_lines before activation
All monetary values stored as NUMERIC(18,2); multi-currency lines store original + exchange_rate + functional_amount
Account balance updates are applied in SERIALIZABLE transaction to prevent race conditions
AuditLog rows are append-only; no UPDATE or DELETE ever issued against audit_logs

hard_constraints: Do NOT generate pages not listed in modules[]
Do NOT invent fields not listed in entities[].fields[]
Do NOT call API routes not listed in api.endpoints
ALWAYS use shared components from components.shared — never create one-off styled versions
ALWAYS enforce role permissions from permissions.matrix at both UI render and API middleware layers
NEVER display raw UUIDs — always resolve to human-readable label
NEVER display raw ISO timestamp strings — always use AbsoluteDate or RelativeDate components

NEVER use untyped or 'any' for API responses. ALWAYS define strict TypeScript interfaces or types. Every API response must be typed with the matching I{Entity} interface from src/types/. Use PaginatedResponse<I{Entity}> for list endpoints.
NEVER call fetch() directly in a component — all calls go through src/api/* service functions
NEVER use useState + useEffect for server data — always use React Query hooks
NEVER scatter role checks inline — always use roleGuard() from src/lib/roleGuard.ts
NEVER use magic strings for routes, query keys, or enum values — import from src/constants/
EVERY currency field MUST use CurrencyDisplay component with org currency — never plain number
EVERY debit/credit column MUST use debit/credit typography tokens from design_system.accounting_specific
EVERY amount input MUST use CurrencyInput component — never plain NumberInput for money
Journal entry POST action MUST be blocked if total_debit != total_credit — enforce in both UI and API
Closed and locked financial periods MUST be read-only everywhere — no edit actions rendered
Inactive accounts MUST NOT appear in account selectors for new transactions
Reconciliation complete action MUST be disabled if difference != 0
EVERY list/table/chart MUST have EmptyState defined
EVERY async fetch MUST show LoadingSkeleton before data arrives
EVERY mutation MUST handle onError with toast
EVERY submit button MUST show loading state and be disabled during in-flight request
EVERY form MUST use React Hook Form v7 + Zod schema validation
Sidebar order MUST match modules[].sidebar_order — never reorder
Default form type is SlideOverForm unless module explicitly specifies FullPage or Modal
StatusBadge MUST NEVER accept a color prop directly — always read color from src/constants/statusColors.ts using entity name + status value as lookup keys

JournalLinesEditor MUST display BalanceIndicator component and MUST disable the Post button when total_debit != total_credit — the imbalance state uses design_system.accounting_specific.reconciliation_difference_nonzero styling
BalanceIndicator MUST use reconciliation_difference_zero class when balanced, reconciliation_difference_nonzero class (red + animate-pulse) when unbalanced — NEVER hardcode colors
EVERY optimistic mutation MUST implement onMutate (apply optimistic update), onError (rollback via context.previousData), and onSettled (always invalidate) — never omit the rollback

EVERY backend repository/service function MUST accept orgId as first parameter. EVERY SQL query MUST include WHERE org_id = :orgId. orgScope middleware MUST be applied to ALL authenticated routes. NEVER write a query without org_id scoping.
accounting_domain_rules: Double-entry rule: every journal entry must have total_debit == total_credit before posting
Closed period rule: no new entries may be posted to closed or locked financial periods
Invoice total rule: invoice.total = sum(line.line_total) + sum(line.tax_amount) rounded to 2 decimal places
Bill total rule: bill.total = sum(line.line_total) + sum(line.tax_amount) rounded to 2 decimal places
Balance due rule: balance_due = total - sum(applied_payment_amounts)
Payment cap rule: payment amount cannot exceed balance_due unless explicitly allowed by org setting
Reconciliation rule: difference = statement_ending_balance - book_ending_balance; must equal 0 to complete
Inactive account rule: accounts with is_active = false must not appear in any transaction selectors
Account uniqueness rule: account code must be unique within org
Period overlap rule: financial period start/end dates must not overlap with other periods in the same org
Void rule: voiding a posted journal entry auto-creates a reversing entry with all debits and credits swapped
Auto-numbering rule: invoice_number, bill_number, payment_number, expense_number, entry_number are auto-generated as ENTITY-YYYYMMDD-NNNN and are immutable after creation
output_requirements: Output complete folder structure matching folder_structure keys
Output every file listed in folder_structure — no placeholder files
All TypeScript interfaces must be complete — no partial or TODO fields
All components must be fully functional — no pseudo-code
All hooks must include proper stale_time, invalidation, and error handling
All Zod schemas must include every validation rule listed in entities[].fields[].validation
All Zustand stores must match the shape defined in state_management.zustand_config.store_definitions
database/schema.sql must include all enum types, all tables, all foreign keys, all indexes
database/seeds/ must include a default chart of accounts and default tax rates
Code must compile with tsc --strict and have zero type errors

domain_rule_enforcement.description: Every accounting_domain_rule has a defined enforcement point. The AI must implement ALL of these — not just the UI-side check.

rules.ui_enforcement: JournalLinesEditor shows BalanceIndicator. Post button disabled when total_debit != total_credit.
rules.api_enforcement: POST /journal-entries/:id/post — server validates sum(debit) == sum(credit) across all lines. Returns 409 CONFLICT with difference amount if unbalanced.
rules.db_enforcement: NONE — balance is application-level, not DB constraint, because lines are inserted incrementally.

rules.ui_enforcement: PeriodSelect filters out closed/locked periods by default. Closed period records show read-only badge. All edit actions hidden.
rules.api_enforcement: All write endpoints (POST /journal-entries, POST /invoices, POST /bills, PATCH /*, POST /payments, POST /expenses) check period_id.status. Returns 409 CONFLICT if period is closed.
rules.db_enforcement: No DB constraint — enforced at application layer only.

rules.ui_enforcement: InvoiceLinesEditor and BillLinesEditor compute totals client-side in real-time for display only. Actual stored values come from server.
rules.api_enforcement: PUT /:id/lines — server recomputes subtotal = sum(quantity * unit_price), tax_total = sum(line * tax_rate.percentage / 100), total = subtotal + tax_total, rounded to 2 decimal places. Client-side computation is display-only.
rules.db_enforcement: NUMERIC(15,2) columns enforce 2 decimal precision at storage.

rules.ui_enforcement: balance_due shown in invoice/bill detail. Read-only — never editable.
rules.api_enforcement: POST /payments — after recording payment, server updates linked invoice/bill: balance_due = total - sum(applied_payments). If balance_due <= 0: set status = 'paid'. If 0 < balance_due < total: set status = 'partial'.
rules.db_enforcement: NUMERIC(15,2) column. Cannot go negative — application enforces payment_cap rule.

rules.ui_enforcement: RecordPaymentSlideOver pre-fills amount with current balance_due. Shows warning if user enters amount > balance_due.
rules.api_enforcement: POST /payments — if amount > invoice.balance_due or bill.balance_due: return 422 unless org_settings.allow_overpayment = true.

rules.ui_enforcement: AccountSelector always passes is_active=true to useAccounts() query. Inactive accounts never appear in dropdowns.
rules.api_enforcement: All endpoints accepting account_id (journal lines, invoice lines, bill lines, expenses) validate account.is_active = true. Returns 422 if inactive.
rules.db_enforcement: Soft delete — accounts are deactivated, not deleted. is_active flag enforced at application layer.

rules.ui_enforcement: Complete button disabled when reconciliationStore.isComplete = false. difference displayed prominently.
rules.api_enforcement: POST /reconciliation-sessions/:id/complete — validates abs(difference) < 0.01. Returns 409 CONFLICT with current difference if not zero.

rules.ui_enforcement: Void action shows confirmation dialog with reason field. After success, navigate to new reversing entry.
rules.api_enforcement: POST /journal-entries/:id/void — creates new journal entry with all line debits and credits swapped. Sets original entry status = voided. Links both entries via reference field.
rules.db_enforcement: CASCADE — lines are managed by application, not DB trigger.

rules.ui_enforcement: Number fields (invoice_number, bill_number, etc.) are hidden from create forms (show_in_form: false). Displayed as read-only after creation.
rules.api_enforcement: POST endpoints for invoice, bill, payment, expense, journal_entry — server generates number using format ENTITY-YYYYMMDD-NNNN where NNNN is zero-padded daily sequence per org. Unique constraint at DB level.

rules.ui_enforcement: FinancialPeriodsPage shows existing period date ranges visually. Validation error shown if dates overlap.
rules.api_enforcement: POST /financial-periods — server checks for date range overlap with existing periods in same org. Returns 409 CONFLICT naming the conflicting period.
rules.db_enforcement: No DB constraint — application-level check via query.

type_generation_rules.description: Rules for generating TypeScript types, interfaces, and Zod schemas from this spec.
rules: Generate one interface file per entity: src/types/{Entity}.ts. Export I{Entity} interface, {Entity}Status enum (if entity has status field), Z{Entity} Zod schema.
I{Entity} interface fields must match every field in entities[].fields[] with correct ts_type. nullable=true fields must be typed as T|null.
Enum fields must use the generated enum type, not string. Example: status: InvoiceStatus, not status: string.
Export a Z{Entity}CreateDTO Zod schema matching api.request_schemas.Create{Entity}DTO for every entity that has a POST endpoint.
Export a Z{Entity}UpdateDTO as ZCreate{Entity}DTO.partial() for every entity that has a PATCH endpoint.
All enums registered in top-level enums{} must be generated in src/types/enums.ts as both a TypeScript const enum and a Zod enum (z.enum([...])).
src/types/index.ts must re-export everything from all entity type files and enums.ts.
Never use 'any' type. Use 'unknown' for polymorphic entity_id references.
generated computed fields (e.g. balance_due, total) must be included as readonly fields in the interface, not in the DTO schemas.

hook_generation_rules.description: Rules for generating React Query hooks (queries and mutations) in src/hooks/.

query_hook_pattern.template: export function use{Entity}s(filters?: {Entity}ListFilters) { return useQuery({ queryKey: [{entity_plural}, orgId, filters], queryFn: () => {entity}Api.list(filters), staleTime: 30_000 }); }
required_options: queryKey (array including orgId + all filter params)
query_hook_pattern.always_include: select: option for transforming data if needed

mutation_hook_pattern.template: export function useCreate{Entity}() { const qc = useQueryClient(); return useMutation({ mutationFn: (data) => {entity}Api.create(data), onSuccess: () => { qc.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['{entity_plural}', orgId] }); toast.success('{Entity} created'); }, onError: (err) => toast.error(getErrorMessage(err)) }); }
required_handlers: onSuccess: invalidate affected query keys + success toast

api_generation_rules.description: Rules for generating the Express API layer: routes, controllers, services, repositories.

file_structure.routes: src/server/routes/{entity}.routes.ts — registers Express router paths, applies authMiddleware and permissionGuard
file_structure.controllers: src/server/controllers/{entity}.controller.ts — parses req, calls service, sends response
file_structure.services: src/server/services/{entity}.service.ts — business logic, domain rule enforcement, transaction management
file_structure.repositories: src/server/repositories/{entity}.repository.ts — SQL queries via pg or drizzle-orm, always includes WHERE org_id = $1

rules: Every route must call authMiddleware before handler. authMiddleware extracts JWT, sets req.user = { id, org_id, role }.
Every route that requires a permission must call permissionGuard(permission) after authMiddleware. permissionGuard checks permissions.matrix[permission][role].
Every repository function MUST accept orgId as first parameter and include it in every WHERE clause.
Every service function MUST enforce domain rules from domain_rule_enforcement[] before any database write.
Every POST/PATCH must parse request body through the corresponding Zod schema from api.request_schemas. Return 422 on validation failure with { error, details } envelope.
Every response must be wrapped in the response_envelope: { data: T, meta: {...} } for lists, { data: T } for single.
Every DELETE returns 204 No Content on success.
Every mutation endpoint must increment the entity's version field (optimistic lock). On version conflict return 409.
Apply RLS via SET LOCAL app.current_org_id = $orgId before every query in a transaction.
Paginate all list endpoints. Default page=1, limit=50, max limit=200. Include total count in meta.
Use database transactions for any operation that modifies more than one table. Pass the transaction client through service → repository.

form_generation_rules.description: Rules for generating React Hook Form + Zod forms from entity field definitions.
rules: Every form uses useForm<ZCreate{Entity}DTO>({ resolver: zodResolver(ZCreate{Entity}DTO), defaultValues }) from react-hook-form.

Map each entity field to the correct input component: db_type NUMERIC/DECIMAL → CurrencyInput; db_type DATE → DatePicker; db_type with enum_values → Select; db_type BOOLEAN → Toggle; db_type TEXT with validation.max > 200 → TextArea; otherwise → standard Input.

Use AccountSelector for all fields named *_account_id. Use CustomerSelector for customer_id. Use VendorSelector for vendor_id. Use TaxRateSelector for tax_rate_id. Use BankAccountSelector for bank_account_id.
All required fields (nullable=false with no default) must have Zod .min(1) or non-optional and display a red asterisk label.
Server validation errors (422 response details map) must be applied to form via Object.entries(details).forEach(([key, msgs]) => form.setError(key, { message: msgs[0] })).
Form submit button: disabled while isSubmitting. Shows Loader2 icon when isSubmitting=true.
SlideOverForm: use onSubmit = form.handleSubmit(async (data) => { await mutateAsync(data); onClose(); }). Always call onClose after success.
Line-item sub-forms (InvoiceLines, BillLines, JournalLines) use useFieldArray. Each row has an Add Line and Remove Line (trash icon) control.
For JournalLinesEditor: render BalanceIndicator beneath lines. Disable Post button when total_debit !== total_credit.
FormField error messages display as text-red-400 text-xs mt-1 below the input.

form_types.SlideOverForm: Default — 90% of create/edit forms. Opens from right. Max width 540px.
form_types.FullPageForm: Used for complex multi-section forms: JournalEntryNewPage, InvoiceNewPage, BillNewPage.
form_types.Modal: Used for single-field forms: rename, add note, set due date.

table_generation_rules.description: Rules for generating DataTable instances from entity field definitions.
rules: Use DataTable component from components.shared. Pass columns[], data, isLoading, emptyState props.
Column generation: include only fields where show_in_table=true in entity.fields[]. Order matches field order in spec.
sortable=true fields get a sort toggle on the column header. Clicking cycles asc → desc → none. Sync to URL ?sort_by=&sort_dir=.
filterable=true fields with filter_type='select' generate a dropdown chip filter in the FilterBar above the table. filterable=true with filter_type='text' generate a SearchInput.
filterable=true with filter_type='date_range' generate a DateRangePicker in the FilterBar.
Currency columns must use CurrencyDisplay component, right-aligned, with org currency.
Status columns must use StatusBadge component with badge_colors from the field definition. Never render raw enum value string.
Date columns must use AbsoluteDate component (shows formatted date) or RelativeDate (shows '3 days ago') depending on context. Due dates use AbsoluteDate. CreatedAt uses RelativeDate.
Every DataTable must define an emptyState: { icon, title, description, primaryAction? }.
Row actions: rendered as a MoreMenu (Ellipsis icon) at end of each row. Include only actions visible to user's role (check permissions.matrix).
Bulk actions: shown in a floating bar above table when selectedRows.length > 0. Include only bulk_actions defined in the module's views[].bulk_actions.
Pagination: show page controls at table bottom. Show 'Showing X–Y of Z records'. Sync page to URL ?page=.

component_generation_rules.description: Rules for generating shared and entity-specific components.
rules: NEVER create a new component if one in components.shared or components.input_components already serves the purpose.
Every new component file starts with: import React from 'react'. Use named exports only — no default exports.
Props interfaces must be typed: export interface {Component}Props { ... }. No implicit any.
Use Tailwind CSS classes only — no inline styles, no CSS modules, no styled-components.
Color values must reference design_system tokens (e.g. bg-slate-900, text-slate-400) — no arbitrary hex values.
Loading state: every data-dependent component must render a Skeleton placeholder matching the approximate shape of the loaded content.
Empty state: every list/table component must render EmptyState when data=[]. EmptyState receives icon, title, description, optional action button.
All interactive elements must have accessible labels: aria-label or visible text. Icon-only buttons must have aria-label.
Use Radix UI primitives for: dropdowns (DropdownMenu), dialogs (Dialog), alerts (AlertDialog), tooltips (Tooltip), select (Select), switch (Switch).
Apply cn() (clsx + tailwind-merge) for conditional class merging — never string concatenation.

shared_component_rules.StatusBadge: Import status color map from src/constants/statusColors.ts. Never hardcode colors. Props: status: string, entity: string (to look up correct color map).
shared_component_rules.CurrencyDisplay: Always shows currency symbol + formatted number. Props: amount: number, currency: string (default from org), colorize: boolean (red for negative). Uses org currency from authStore.
shared_component_rules.DataTable: Generic typed component DataTable<T>. Columns defined as ColumnDef<T>[]. Handles sorting, pagination, row selection, and bulk actions.
shared_component_rules.SlideOverForm: Wraps Radix Dialog with slide-in animation. Traps focus. Warns on close if form is dirty. Props: title, description, children, isOpen, onClose, width.
shared_component_rules.BalanceIndicator: Shows debit total, credit total, difference. Uses reconciliation_difference_zero class when balanced, reconciliation_difference_nonzero when not.
shared_component_rules.AbsoluteDate: Formats ISO date string using date-fns format(). Shows full date on hover tooltip (RelativeDate does the inverse).

file_generation_map.description: Maps every output file to the spec section it is generated from. AI must generate ALL files listed here.

test_generation_rules.description: Rules for generating tests. Required test coverage per the output_requirements.

rules: Every hook file must have a corresponding test file: src/__tests__/unit/hooks/use{Entity}(s).test.ts
Every service file must have a test: src/__tests__/unit/services/{entity}.service.test.ts
Test accounting domain rules: double-entry balance, period lock, payment cap, reconciliation difference=0
Mock API calls with msw (Mock Service Worker) — no real network calls in unit tests
Test StatusBadge renders correct color for each enum value using badge_colors from spec
Every API endpoint must have an integration test: method, path, auth, expected response shape
Test RLS: requests with org_id=A must never return data belonging to org_id=B
Test permission guards: 403 for unauthorized roles, 401 for missing token
Test domain rule enforcement: posting unbalanced journal entry returns 422
Test optimistic locking: concurrent PATCH with stale version returns 409

critical_flows: Create Invoice → Send → Record Payment → Verify AR balance updated
Create Bill → Approve → Record Payment → Verify AP balance updated
Create Journal Entry → Post → Verify Trial Balance updated
Import Bank Transactions → Run Auto-Match → Confirm Match → Complete Reconciliation
Admin invites user → User accepts invite → User logs in with correct role

error_handling_rules.description: Rules for error handling generation across frontend and backend.
backend_rules: All route handlers must be wrapped in asyncHandler middleware — never use try/catch in route files
Use centralised error handler Express middleware registered LAST: app.use(errorHandler)
Throw typed AppError instances: throw new AppError(400, 'VALIDATION_ERROR', 'message', details)
Never leak stack traces, internal paths, or DB error messages to API response in production
Log full error (including stack) server-side before sending sanitised response to client
Zod validation failures produce 400 with error.details as field → message[] map
Prisma/DB unique violation (P2002) → 409 Conflict with human-readable message
Business rule violations → 422 Unprocessable Entity with specific error code
frontend_rules: All React Query mutations have onError callback that calls toast.error(error.message)
All page-level queries have error boundary — render EmptyState variant='error' on error
Axios response interceptor handles 401 (token refresh) and 500 (log + generic toast)
Form mutations: map error.details to React Hook Form setError() for field-level display
Network errors (no response): show 'Connection lost' toast with retry button
Never show error.code to end users — only error.message
Log errors to console.error in development; send to error tracking service (e.g. Sentry) in production

loading_state_rules.description: Rules for generating loading states consistently.
rules: Every list page query renders SkeletonTable (matching final column count) while isLoading=true
Every detail page renders SkeletonCard for entity header while isLoading=true
Dashboard stat cards render SkeletonCard (lines=2) while data loads
Chart components render a Skeleton rectangle of matching height while loading
Mutation buttons: set disabled={isPending} and show <Loader2 className='animate-spin' /> inside button while mutation is in flight
Never show full-page loading overlay for data refetches — only for initial load
isLoading (true only on first fetch) vs isFetching (true on any fetch including refetch) — use isLoading for skeleton display, isFetching for subtle spinner only
Use React.Suspense + lazy() for heavy page components — pair with ErrorBoundary

routing_rules.description: Rules for generating React Router v6 routes and navigation.
rules: Use createBrowserRouter with data loaders — not BrowserRouter with useEffect fetching
Wrap all authenticated routes in AuthGuard component that checks authStore.isAuthenticated
Wrap module routes in PermissionGuard component that checks usePermission(module.permissions[0])
Use Outlet for nested route layouts — AppShell wraps all authenticated routes
Lazy-load all page components with React.lazy() — one chunk per route
Use useNavigate for programmatic navigation — never window.location
URL search params for all filter/sort/page state — use useSearchParams hook
Define all route paths as constants in src/lib/routes.ts — never hardcode strings in components
Implement 404 catch-all route rendering NotFoundPage
Redirect /  → /dashboard if authenticated; → /login if not

auth_rules.description: Rules for generating authentication and session management code.
rules: JWT access token stored in memory (authStore.accessToken) — NEVER in localStorage or sessionStorage
Refresh token stored in HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie — never accessible from JS
On app init: call GET /api/auth/me with credentials:include — if 200, populate authStore; if 401, redirect to /login
Silent token refresh: axios interceptor catches 401, calls POST /api/auth/refresh, retries original request once
On logout: call POST /api/auth/logout (clears refresh token cookie), then clear authStore and React Query cache
Protect all API routes with authMiddleware that validates JWT and injects req.user and req.orgId
All DB queries must include WHERE org_id = req.orgId — enforced by global_query_filters RLS policy
Never log access tokens, refresh tokens, or passwords — even in development
Password hashing: bcrypt with cost factor 12
Session expiry: access token 15m, refresh token 7d with rotation

realtime_rules.description: Rules for generating real-time WebSocket functionality.
rules: Socket.io server initialised alongside Express — share same HTTP server instance
All socket connections authenticated: validate JWT in socket handshake middleware before allowing connection
On successful auth: auto-join rooms org:{org_id} and user:{user_id}
Emit events server-side from service layer (not route handlers) after successful DB mutation
Event payload must match the events section schema exactly
Client wsStore handles reconnection with socket.io built-in reconnection (exponential backoff, max 5 attempts)
On reconnect: invalidate all React Query active queries to catch missed updates
Never broadcast sensitive data (PII, financial details) over WebSocket — emit entity IDs only for cache invalidation triggers when in doubt
Socket rooms are org-scoped — server validates room join authorization before emitting

migration_rules.tool: Prisma Migrate (prisma migrate dev for dev, prisma migrate deploy for prod)
rules: One migration file per logical change — never bundle unrelated schema changes
Migration files are immutable once committed — never edit existing migrations

All new tables must include: id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(), deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
All FK columns must have a corresponding index (see indexes in entities section)
Enum types: use pg_type from enums section — CREATE TYPE IF NOT EXISTS
RLS policies: generate ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + CREATE POLICY for every new table using global_query_filters pattern
Seed file: prisma/seed.ts — seed default chart of accounts, tax rates, and financial periods for new orgs
Never run raw SQL in application code — all schema changes via migrations
Soft delete: never use CASCADE DELETE on FK constraints — use SET NULL or RESTRICT; handle in application layer

seed_data_rules.description: Rules for generating seed data for new organizations.
seed_data_rules.trigger: Automatically runs during onboarding flow (job: close_financial_period precondition: new org setup)

default_financial_period.description: Create current month as first open period during onboarding
default_financial_period.name: MMMM YYYY (e.g. January 2025)

rules: Seed data is created within a DB transaction — rollback all on error
Seed is idempotent — check for existing seed data before inserting
Seed accounts are owned by the new org (org_id set correctly)
Do not seed sample invoices, bills, or transactions — only reference data

required_env_vars.DATABASE_URL: PostgreSQL connection string with connection pooling (PgBouncer recommended)
required_env_vars.FRONTEND_URL: For CORS and email link generation

health_check.used_by: Load balancer health check; Docker HEALTHCHECK

rules: Never commit .env files — use .env.example with all keys and empty values
Run prisma migrate deploy (not prisma migrate dev) in production
Enable PostgreSQL connection pooling in production (max 20 connections per app instance)
Set Content-Security-Policy headers in production nginx config
Enable gzip compression in nginx for all API and static responses
Frontend static assets: long-cache (1 year) with content-hash filenames; HTML files: no-cache

buttons.primary: bg-[var(--accent)] hover:bg-[var(--accent-hover)] text-[var(--text-primary)] font-medium px-4 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm
buttons.secondary: border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-body)] hover:bg-[var(--bg-surface-2)] px-4 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm
buttons.danger: bg-[var(--danger)] hover:bg-[var(--danger-hover)] text-[var(--text-primary)] px-4 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm
buttons.ghost: text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--bg-surface-2)] px-2 py-1 rounded-md text-sm
buttons.loading_state: opacity-75 cursor-not-allowed — replace icon with Loader2 animate-spin

tailwind_config.notes: Run `npx tailwind init -p` to generate postcss.config.js. PostCSS + Vite plugin handles purge in prod. All custom class names come from this config — no arbitrary values in component files.

css_variables.usage: All components reference CSS variables via Tailwind's arbitrary value syntax: bg-[var(--bg-surface)]. Do not hardcode color classes in components — always use design token aliases.

badge_color_tokens.description: Semantic token names used in entities[].fields[].badge_colors. StatusBadge component resolves these to Tailwind classes via src/constants/statusColors.ts.

animations.description: Animation tokens and Tailwind CSS animation classes. All durations respect prefers-reduced-motion.

easings.ease_in_out: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) — elements moving within the screen

icons.description: Icon system conventions. All icons from lucide-react.

sizes.sm: 16px / size-4  — default for buttons and inline use
sizes.lg: 24px / size-6  — page header icons, empty state icons

icons.color: Inherit from parent text color (currentColor) — do not hardcode icon colors

form_layout.description: Standard form layout patterns for the application.

page_form.description: Full-page form for complex entities (Invoice, Bill, JournalEntry) — used for create and edit
page_form.layout: Two-column grid (lg:grid-cols-3) — main content left (col-span-2), sidebar right (col-span-1)
page_form.main_content: FormSection components stacked vertically with gap-y-6

modal_form.description: Modal-based form for simple entities (Account, TaxRate, Customer, Vendor) — create and quick-edit

slide_over_form.description: SlideOver panel for in-context edits (Expense on a Bill detail page, Payment on Invoice)
slide_over_form.layout: Single column, scrollable body, sticky footer with Cancel + Submit

inline_form.description: Inline add-row form within line-item tables (InvoiceLines, BillLines, JournalLines)
inline_form.layout: Table row with input cells; Add Row button spans full width
inline_form.save_trigger: Tab-to-next-row adds row implicitly; delete via Trash icon

field_widths.half_width: dates, status, currency, country, email, phone
field_widths.fixed_sm: w-24 — percentage inputs, short code fields

page_layout.description: Standard page layout structure used across all pages.

shell.sidebar: SidebarNav — fixed left, width 240px expanded / 60px collapsed
shell.topbar: h-14 border-b border-[var(--border)] flex items-center px-6 shrink-0

page_regions.page_header: PageHeader component — title, breadcrumb, primary action button
page_regions.filter_bar: FilterBar component — search input, filter chips, date range, sort (sticky below page header)
page_regions.pagination_bar: Bottom of content area — page info, page size selector, prev/next buttons

detail_page_layout.header: Entity title, status badge, action buttons (Edit, Void, Post etc.), breadcrumb
detail_page_layout.body: Two-column: main details left (lg:col-span-2), related entities right (lg:col-span-1)
detail_page_layout.tabs: If entity has many subsections (Invoice Lines, Payments, Attachments, Audit Trail) — use Tabs component

responsive_breakpoints.mobile: < 768px: sidebar hidden (drawer), single column layout, condensed table with horizontal scroll
responsive_breakpoints.tablet: 768–1279px: sidebar collapsed (icons only), two-column layout where possible

data_display_patterns.description: Standardised patterns for displaying financial and entity data.

currency_display.large_numbers: Use compact notation (1.2M) only on dashboard stat cards, never in financial tables

date_display.standard: MMM D, YYYY (e.g. Jan 15, 2025) — use date-fns format(date, 'MMM d, yyyy')
date_display.relative: X days ago / in X days — notifications and overdue badges only, not in financial tables
date_display.timezone: Always display in org.timezone setting; store/transmit as UTC

status_display.always_show: In list tables and detail page headers
status_display.interactive: In detail page, StatusBadge may have dropdown for allowed status transitions

null_empty_display.text_fields: Show em-dash (—) for null text values in tables
null_empty_display.amount_fields: Show — for null amounts; never show 0 for null
null_empty_display.relation_fields: Show — if FK is null (e.g. no vendor assigned)

table_columns.id_column: Never show raw UUID in tables — use entity-specific identifier (invoice_number, code, name)
table_columns.amount_columns: Right-align all amount columns; use AmountCell component
table_columns.action_column: Right-align; sticky on horizontal scroll; show Edit icon (primary action) + '...' menu for secondary actions

icon_usage.description: When and how to use icons — referencing the icons subsection definitions.
rules: Use entity_icon_map icons in: sidebar nav, page headers, empty states, entity type labels
Use action_icon_map icons in: button labels (icon + text), icon-only buttons (with tooltip), table action columns
Icon-only buttons MUST have aria-label and Tooltip component
Never use icons without meaning — every icon must be paired with either visible text or aria-label
Colored icons: only use color tokens (text-[var(--success)], text-[var(--danger)]) — never hardcoded Tailwind colors
Loading state: replace icon with Loader2 with animate-spin class; do not use a separate spinner element

frontend.forms: React Hook Form v7 + Zod validation

multi_tenancy.description: org_id is NEVER sent by the client. It is extracted from the JWT token by the org_id middleware and applied to every database query automatically. No API endpoint accepts or returns org_id.

db_schema.org_id_column: UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
db_schema.notes: Every table EXCEPT organizations has an org_id column. All queries include WHERE org_id = :orgId from middleware.

middleware.name: orgScope
middleware.behavior: Extracts org_id from verified JWT. Attaches req.orgId. Applied to ALL authenticated routes. Every repository/service function accepts orgId as a required parameter — never reads from global state.
middleware.enforcement: If org_id is missing from JWT: return 401. If org_id does not match any organization: return 401.

indexes.pattern: Every major table has a composite index on (org_id, <primary_sort_field>). Example: CREATE INDEX idx_invoices_org_status ON invoices(org_id, status) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL.
indexes.notes: Queries ALWAYS filter by org_id first. Never allow cross-org data access.

multi_tenancy.entity_field_note: org_id IS present in entities[].fields[] and in global_fields because it is a real database column on every table. However, org_id is NEVER sent by the client in any API request body, and NEVER needs to be provided by frontend code. It is extracted from the JWT claims by the orgScope middleware and injected into every query automatically. The frontend never reads or writes org_id.
multi_tenancy.frontend_note: The frontend never sends org_id. The org object (name, settings) is available in authStore.org but is only used for display — never sent as a query parameter.
multi_tenancy.generation_rule: EVERY backend repository function must accept orgId as its first parameter. EVERY SQL query must include WHERE org_id = :orgId. The orgScope middleware must be applied before EVERY authenticated route handler. NEVER allow a query that could return data across organizations.

jobs.name: recalculate_account_balances
jobs.description: Recomputes current_balance for all accounts by summing posted journal line debits and credits. Writes to accounts.current_balance cache column.
jobs.implementation_notes: Use a single SQL UPDATE … FROM (SELECT …) subquery scoped to org_id. Lock level: row-level advisory lock per org_id.

jobs.name: recalculate_bank_account_balances
jobs.description: Recomputes current_balance for all bank accounts: opening_balance + sum(credit transactions) - sum(debit transactions).
jobs.implementation_notes: Group bank transactions by bank_account_id and direction. Filter to non-deleted rows.

jobs.name: mark_invoices_overdue
jobs.description: Scans all invoices where status IN ('sent','partial') AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE. Updates status to 'overdue' and emits invoice.overdue events.
jobs.implementation_notes: Batch update in chunks of 500. Emit invoice.overdue event per updated invoice for downstream notification handling.

jobs.name: mark_bills_overdue
jobs.description: Scans all bills where status IN ('received','approved','partial') AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE. Updates status to 'overdue'.

jobs.name: run_recurring_transactions
jobs.description: Generates new journal entries from recurring_template records where next_run_date <= CURRENT_DATE and is_active = true. Advances next_run_date according to recurrence rule.
jobs.implementation_notes: Use idempotency key = (template_id, next_run_date) to prevent duplicate entries on retry. Emit journal_entry.created event after each creation.

jobs.name: cleanup_soft_deleted_records
jobs.description: Hard-deletes records where deleted_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days', respecting retention policy. Excludes AuditLog (never hard-deleted). Processes tables in FK-safe order.
jobs.implementation_notes: Process in batches of 1000. Run inside a transaction. Write a cleanup_run audit log entry. Never hard-delete AuditLog, Organization, or User records.

jobs.name: send_payment_reminders
jobs.description: Sends email/notification reminders for invoices overdue by 1, 7, 14, and 30 days. Respects org-level reminder settings and per-customer opt-out.
jobs.implementation_notes: Group by reminder tier (1d, 7d, 14d, 30d). Use notification.type = 'payment_reminder'. Check org settings before sending.

jobs.name: close_financial_period
jobs.description: Locks a financial period: validates all journal entries are posted or voided, generates closing entries for P&L accounts, transitions period status to 'locked'.
jobs.implementation_notes: Run in a single database transaction. Reject if any draft journal entries remain open. Emit financial_period.closed event on completion.

jobs.name: sync_bank_transactions
jobs.description: Fetches new transactions from connected bank accounts via Plaid/open-banking API. Deduplicates by external_transaction_id. Creates BankTransaction records.
jobs.implementation_notes: Per-bank-account lock. Emit bank_transaction.imported event per batch. Store raw payload in metadata column.

jobs.name: generate_financial_reports
jobs.description: Pre-computes and caches Profit & Loss, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow reports for the current and previous period. Stores results in report_cache table with expiry.
jobs.implementation_notes: Cache key = (org_id, report_type, period_id). Invalidate on journal_entry.posted event. TTL = 24 hours.

account_created.triggered_when: A new Account record is persisted (POST /api/accounts returns 201)

journal_entry_created.triggered_when: A new JournalEntry with status='draft' is persisted

journal_entry_posted.triggered_when: JournalEntry.status transitions from 'draft' to 'posted' via POST /api/journal-entries/:id/post

invoice_created.triggered_when: A new Invoice record is persisted (POST /api/invoices returns 201)

invoice_sent.triggered_when: POST /api/invoices/:id/send succeeds — invoice emailed to customer and status transitions to 'sent'

invoice_paid.triggered_when: Invoice.status transitions to 'paid' — triggered by payment application that reduces balance_due to 0

bill_created.triggered_when: A new Bill record is persisted (POST /api/bills returns 201)

payment_received.triggered_when: A new Payment with direction='inbound' is persisted (POST /api/payments returns 201)

payment_made.triggered_when: A new Payment with direction='outbound' is persisted (POST /api/payments returns 201)

payment_applied.triggered_when: POST /api/payments/:id/apply — a PaymentApplication record is created linking a payment to an invoice or bill

expense_submitted.triggered_when: Expense.status transitions from 'draft' to 'submitted' via PATCH /api/expenses/:id

bank_transaction_imported.triggered_when: job:sync_bank_transactions imports one or more BankTransaction records

financial_period_closed.triggered_when: job:close_financial_period completes successfully and FinancialPeriod.status becomes 'locked'

user_invited.triggered_when: POST /api/auth/invite — a new User record with status='invited' is created

attachment_uploaded.triggered_when: POST /api/attachments — a new Attachment record is persisted after S3 upload confirmation

_meta.note: All schemas are exported as named Zod objects. Inferred TypeScript types use z.infer<typeof SchemaName>.
_meta.dual_schema_note: This section (validation_schemas) defines FRONTEND Zod schemas for React Hook Form validation — they run in the browser before submission. The api.request_schemas section defines BACKEND DTO schemas (also Zod) used by the Express server for request body validation. Both sections must stay in sync. Field names in both sections MUST exactly match the corresponding entity fields[] names.

AccountCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ code: z.string().min(1).max(20), name: z.string().min(1).max(255), type: z.enum(['asset','liability','equity','revenue','expense','contra']), subtype: z.string().max(100).optional(), description: z.string().max(500).optional(), parent_account_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), is_active: z.boolean().default(true) })

code.notes: Unique per org — enforced at DB level

subtype.notes: Maps to Account.subtype — e.g. current_asset, fixed_asset

refinements: Parent account must belong to same org (validated server-side)
Account code must be unique within org (validated server-side)

AccountUpdate.zod_schema: z.object({ code: z.string().min(1).max(20).optional(), name: z.string().min(1).max(255).optional(), subtype: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), description: z.string().max(500).optional().nullable(), parent_account_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), is_active: z.boolean().optional() })
refinements: type field is immutable after creation

JournalEntryCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ reference: z.string().min(1).max(255), date: z.string().date(), description: z.string().max(500).optional(), period_id: z.string().uuid(), lines: z.array(z.object({ account_id: z.string().uuid(), debit: z.number().min(0).default(0), credit: z.number().min(0).default(0), description: z.string().max(255).optional(), tax_rate_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable() })).min(2) }).superRefine((val, ctx) => { const totalDebit = val.lines.reduce((s,l) => s+l.debit, 0); const totalCredit = val.lines.reduce((s,l) => s+l.credit, 0); if (Math.abs(totalDebit - totalCredit) > 0.001) ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', message: 'Journal entry must balance: total debits must equal total credits', path: ['lines'] }); })

date.notes: ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) — maps to JournalEntry.date

refinements: SUM(lines[*].debit) MUST equal SUM(lines[*].credit) — enforced in superRefine
Each line: debit > 0 XOR credit > 0 (both zero or both non-zero is invalid)
period_id must reference an open FinancialPeriod (validated server-side)
All account_ids must belong to same org (validated server-side)

JournalEntryUpdate.zod_schema: z.object({ reference: z.string().min(1).max(255).optional(), date: z.string().date().optional(), description: z.string().max(500).optional().nullable(), period_id: z.string().uuid().optional(), lines: z.array(JournalLineInputSchema).min(2).optional() }).superRefine(/* balance check if lines provided */)
refinements: Only draft entries can be updated — posted/voided entries are immutable

InvoiceCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ customer_id: z.string().uuid(), invoice_number: z.string().min(1).max(50).optional(), invoice_date: z.string().date(), due_date: z.string().date(), period_id: z.string().uuid(), currency: z.string().length(3).default('USD'), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional(), lines: z.array(z.object({ description: z.string().min(1).max(255), quantity: z.number().positive(), unit_price: z.number().min(0), revenue_account_id: z.string().uuid(), tax_rate_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable() })).min(1) })
refinements: due_date must be >= invoice_date
customer_id must belong to same org
period_id must be an open FinancialPeriod

InvoiceUpdate.zod_schema: z.object({ invoice_date: z.string().date().optional(), due_date: z.string().date().optional(), period_id: z.string().uuid().optional(), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional().nullable(), lines: z.array(InvoiceLineInputSchema).min(1).optional() })
refinements: Only draft invoices can be updated

BillCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ vendor_id: z.string().uuid(), bill_number: z.string().min(1).max(50).optional(), bill_date: z.string().date(), due_date: z.string().date(), period_id: z.string().uuid(), currency: z.string().length(3).default('USD'), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional(), lines: z.array(z.object({ description: z.string().min(1).max(255), quantity: z.number().positive(), unit_cost: z.number().min(0), expense_account_id: z.string().uuid(), tax_rate_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable() })).min(1) })
refinements: due_date >= bill_date
vendor_id must belong to same org

CustomerCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).max(255), email: z.string().email().optional().nullable(), phone: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable(), company_name: z.string().max(150).optional().nullable(), billing_address_line1: z.string().max(200).optional().nullable(), billing_address_line2: z.string().max(200).optional().nullable(), billing_city: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), billing_state: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), billing_postal_code: z.string().max(20).optional().nullable(), billing_country: z.string().length(2).optional().nullable(), currency: z.string().length(3).default('USD'), payment_terms_days: z.number().int().min(0).max(365).default(30), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional().nullable(), tax_number: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable() })
refinements: email must be unique per org if provided (validated server-side)

CustomerUpdate.zod_schema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).max(255).optional(), email: z.string().email().optional().nullable(), phone: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable(), company_name: z.string().max(150).optional().nullable(), billing_address_line1: z.string().max(200).optional().nullable(), billing_address_line2: z.string().max(200).optional().nullable(), billing_city: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), billing_state: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), billing_postal_code: z.string().max(20).optional().nullable(), billing_country: z.string().length(2).optional().nullable(), currency: z.string().length(3).optional(), payment_terms_days: z.number().int().min(0).max(365).optional(), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional().nullable(), tax_number: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable(), is_active: z.boolean().optional() })

VendorCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).max(255), email: z.string().email().optional().nullable(), phone: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable(), company_name: z.string().max(150).optional().nullable(), address_line1: z.string().max(200).optional().nullable(), address_line2: z.string().max(200).optional().nullable(), city: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), state: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), postal_code: z.string().max(20).optional().nullable(), country: z.string().length(2).optional().nullable(), currency: z.string().length(3).default('USD'), payment_terms_days: z.number().int().min(0).max(365).default(30), default_expense_account_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), tax_number: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable(), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional().nullable() })
refinements: default_expense_account_id must reference an account with type='expense' (validated server-side)

VendorUpdate.zod_schema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).max(255).optional(), email: z.string().email().optional().nullable(), phone: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable(), company_name: z.string().max(150).optional().nullable(), currency: z.string().length(3).optional(), payment_terms_days: z.number().int().min(0).max(365).optional(), default_expense_account_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), tax_number: z.string().max(50).optional().nullable(), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional().nullable(), is_active: z.boolean().optional() })

PaymentCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ direction: z.enum(['inbound','outbound']), amount: z.number().positive(), currency: z.string().length(3).default('USD'), payment_date: z.string().date(), payment_method: z.enum(['bank_transfer','credit_card','debit_card','cash','check','stripe','paypal','ach','wire','other']), reference: z.string().max(255).optional().nullable(), notes: z.string().max(500).optional().nullable(), bank_account_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), customer_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), vendor_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), invoice_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), bill_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable() }).superRefine((val, ctx) => { if (val.direction === 'inbound' && !val.customer_id) ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', message: 'customer_id required for inbound payments', path: ['customer_id'] }); if (val.direction === 'outbound' && !val.vendor_id) ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', message: 'vendor_id required for outbound payments', path: ['vendor_id'] }); })
refinements: customer_id required when direction='inbound'
vendor_id required when direction='outbound'
invoice_id must belong to customer_id when provided
bill_id must belong to vendor_id when provided

ExpenseCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ expense_date: z.string().date(), amount: z.number().positive(), currency: z.string().length(3).default('USD'), description: z.string().min(1).max(255), account_id: z.string().uuid(), vendor_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), tax_rate_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), period_id: z.string().uuid(), receipt_attachment_id: z.string().uuid().optional().nullable(), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional().nullable() })
refinements: account_id must reference an account with type='expense'

BankAccountCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).max(255), account_type: z.enum(['checking','savings','credit_card','line_of_credit','investment','other']), currency: z.string().length(3).default('USD'), opening_balance: z.number().default(0), opening_balance_date: z.string().date(), institution_name: z.string().max(100).optional().nullable(), account_number_last4: z.string().length(4).optional().nullable()).optional().nullable(), gl_account_id: z.string().uuid() })
refinements: gl_account_id must reference an account with type='asset'

TaxRateCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).max(100), percentage: z.number().min(0).max(100), type: z.enum(['sales','vat','gst','hst','withholding','other'])  /* maps to TaxRate.type (pg_type: tax_type) */, description: z.string().max(500).optional().nullable(), is_compound: z.boolean().default(false), is_active: z.boolean().default(true) })

ReconciliationSessionCreate.zod_schema: z.object({ bank_account_id: z.string().uuid(), start_date: z.string().date(), end_date: z.string().date(), statement_ending_balance: z.number() }).superRefine((val, ctx) => { if (val.end_date < val.start_date) ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', message: 'end_date must be >= start_date', path: ['end_date'] }); })
refinements: end_date must be >= start_date
bank_account_id must belong to same org
No other in_progress session may exist for the same bank_account_id

UserInvite.zod_schema: z.object({ email: z.string().email(), role: z.enum(['admin','accountant','bookkeeper','manager','viewer']), first_name: z.string().min(1).max(100).optional(), last_name: z.string().min(1).max(100).optional() })
refinements: email must not already exist within org

PasswordReset.zod_schema: z.object({ token: z.string().min(1), password: z.string().min(8), confirm_password: z.string().min(8) }).superRefine((val, ctx) => { if (val.password !== val.confirm_password) ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', message: 'Passwords do not match', path: ['confirm_password'] }); })
refinements: password must equal confirm_password

AttachmentUpload.zod_schema: z.object({ entity_type: z.enum(['invoice','bill','expense','journal_entry','payment','bank_transaction']), entity_id: z.string().uuid(), file_name: z.string().min(1).max(500), mime_type: z.string().min(1).max(255), file_size_bytes: z.number().int().positive().max(26214400) })
refinements: entity_id must reference an existing entity of entity_type within same org
